channel.title: Pike Place Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Pike Place Events

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Market to Table Tour & Cooking Class
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Atrium Kitchen, 93 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

“We wish we did this at the beginning of our trip!” “You’ve exceeded all of our expectations! And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Improv Greenhouse
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Get ready to be surprised\, delighted\, and maybe even see your wildest idea take on a life of its own! Graduate Greenhouse is a Wednesday night showcase featuring alumni from Unexpected Productions Improv School. Each performance highlights a different alumni group\, putting their training to the test with unpredictable scenes\, various formats\, and improvised stories. From long-form storytelling to character work\, these performers explore the boundaries of improv. Jan 7 Market Improv Jan 14 Jan 21 But the fun doesn’t stop there! Stick around for a double feature as we roll into Seattle’s longest-running Improv Open Mic: the Duos Open Mic! Starting at 8:00 pm\, this weekly showcase promises even more laughs\, surprises\, and improv magic.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: 700 Level Student Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Witness the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic by 700 level class.\n\n\n\nJoin the advanced students in our 700 class and witness them blossom with new improv skills. Under the guidance of our expert improv teachers\, 700 students are learning new narrative formats and enhancing their skills by working in an ensemble. You get to see the fruits of their efforts by witnessing the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Showcase 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions\, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious\, fast-paced\, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts\, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. \nAt Duos\, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment\, take risks\, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes\, surprising twists\, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. \nWhether you dream of being the next Key & Peele\, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler\, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine\, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend\, form a team\, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo\, email duoshowcase@gmail.com. \nLooking for a midweek serotonin boost? Watching the show is just as fun as performing in it. Grab a seat\, grab a friend\, and discover why Unexpected Productions is the home of Seattle improv.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Improv Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Wednesdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Seattle’s only open mic for improv. Two-person improv teams! Double your pleasure, double the fun. Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious, fast-paced, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. At Duos, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment, take risks, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes, surprising twists, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. Whether you dream of being the next Key & Peele, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend, form a team, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo, email duoshowcase@gmail.com . Looki

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Market to Table Tour & Cooking Class
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Atrium Kitchen, 93 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

“We wish we did this at the beginning of our trip!” “You’ve exceeded all of our expectations! And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Meet Me @HistoryLink: Wine Tasting
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: HistoryLink Office, 93 Pike St. Suite 315B, Seattle, WA, 98101

The second event in our Meet Me @HistoryLink series will feature a tasting of several wines from Long Shadows Vintners, hosted by Gilles Nicault and David LeClaire. Taste a sampling of some of Washington's finest wines and learn what makes Washington such a great environment for grape growing. Purchase tickets here

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons 2026
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting\, unexpected\, a little dangerous\, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. \nPart One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance\, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire\, and the improvisers will ignite the stage\, bringing your ideas to life. \nPart Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion\, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song\, a monologue\, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories\, surprising twists\, and big comedic swings. \nLoose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas\, fearless performers\, and the thrill of not knowing what happens next. You light the fuse. They run with it. What they create is entirely up to you.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons Improv
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Thursdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting, unexpected, a little dangerous, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. Part One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire, and the improvisers will ignite the stage, bringing your ideas to life. Part Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song, a monologue, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories, surprising twists, and big comedic swings. Loose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas, fearless performers, and the thrill of not knowing what hap

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Insider’s Breakfast and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Get ahead of peak crowds and join Savor Seattle as they take you behind the scenes on an early-morning exploration of Pike Place Market. Their guides on this tour are deeply rooted members of the Market community who will truly take you to “Meet The Producer” using their years of experience in the neighborhood. This is an experience you cannot get with any regular tour guide! Feel like a local as you walk and eat your way through the best morning bites! Learn the names and stories of the people who operate the businesses Seattle Savor's is honored to highlight as their guides give you the full history of how Pike Place became The Soul of Seattle. "Watching the Market come to life is a time-honored Seattle tradition we would love for you to witness as we share our years of expertise with you. Come savor mornings at Pike Place Market!" Tours are available on Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8am - 10:30pm Book a Tour

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Market to Table Tour & Cooking Class
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Atrium Kitchen, 93 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

“We wish we did this at the beginning of our trip!” “You’ve exceeded all of our expectations! And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Improv Happy Hour
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Join us for Improv Happy Hour—where comedy is unscripted, unpredictable, and unbelievably fun! From movie mashups to literary spoofs and bold, experimental comedy, our quick-thinking performers turn your suggestions into hilarious scenes on the spot. This family-friendly show at the iconic Gum Wall in Pike Place Market guarantees a night of laughter, surprises, and improv magic you won’t forget. Bring your wildest ideas, your loudest laughs, and get ready for a one-of-a-kind comedy experience—because at Improv Happy Hour, anything can happen (and usually does)! Buy Tickets

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Seattle Suites: An Improvised Neil Simon Comedy
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Improvised comedy in the style of Neil Simon\, where one hotel suite becomes a comedic pressure cooker for 3 hilarious improvised tales One hotel suite. Countless misunderstandings. Relentless laughs. Seattle Suite is a laugh-driven\, fully improvised play inspired by the style of Plaza Suite and California Suite\, capturing the signature humor of Neil Simon that is fast\, verbal\, and rooted in beautifully human flaws. Each night\, a single Seattle hotel room becomes the setting for interconnected scenes inspired by your suggestions\, friends overshare and spiral\, romantic gestures go wrong\, awkward silences stretch\, and small problems become big ones. The comedy comes from character and relationship\, building through sharp dialogue\, escalating tension\, and recognizable\, lived-in situations. Perfect for fans of smart comedy\, relationship humor\, and classic theatrical wit.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Theatresports 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast\, funny\, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show\, a high-energy\, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle\, part game night\, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show\, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head\, performing games\, stories\, characters\, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds\, decide the winners\, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking\, bold choices\, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show\, unless your parents were super cool.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Dune Moss presented by The Triple Door at The Rabbit Box
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Rabbit Box, 94 Pike Street Unit 11

Dune Moss presented by The Triple Door at The Rabbit Box Friday, April 10th / Doors 6:30pm / Music begins @ 7:30pm $20 General // $25 Advance Dune Moss is a woodland fairy who creates music that blends ethereal magic, indie electronic, and art pop. Her music is inspired by artists like Enya, AURORA, and Imogen Heap, and aims to transport listeners to another world. Moss grew up in a creative household and was inspired by her mother's "majikal" playlist of movie soundtracks and other songs. She plays the accordion and sings and also designs and sells fairy wear on Etsy. Buy Tickets

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: No Strings Attached: An Improvised Puppet Show for Grownups
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s ORIGINAL improvised puppet show for grownups. An improvised fever dream with googly eyes and lots of laughter At Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater\, puppets let their hair down (or strings loose) in No Strings Attached—a hilarious\, adults improv puppet show. After a long day entertaining the kids\, these puppets are ready to play—singing bawdy songs\, cracking jokes\, and getting up to all kinds of outrageous fun. And the best part? You call the shots with your suggestions! Don’t miss this one-of-a-kind comedy show at Pike Place Market. It’s puppets\, it’s improv\, and it’s guaranteed to make you laugh. THIS SHOW CONTAINS MATURE CONTENT\, LANGUAGE\, AND SITUATIONS

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Taste of Improv - Improv Open Mic Georgetown Art Attack
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Seattle Improv School\, 5700 6th Ave S\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Whether you’re a student\, a pro\, or just checking out the Art Attack\, this is your chance to take the stage\, make bold choices\, and have a blast doing it.\n\nJoin Unexpected Productions Improv School as we join Georgetown’s ART ATTACK with a spontaneous showcase of improv talent. Bring your squad\, support each other\, and celebrate the joy of live\, unfiltered performance.\nHit the Georgetown Art Attack\, then head over to the Improv School.\nCome for the art. Stay for the improv. See you there!

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Suites: An Improvised Neil Simon Comedy
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Improvised comedy in the style of Neil Simon\, where one hotel suite becomes a comedic pressure cooker for 3 hilarious improvised tales One hotel suite. Countless misunderstandings. Relentless laughs. Seattle Suite is a laugh-driven\, fully improvised play inspired by the style of Plaza Suite and California Suite\, capturing the signature humor of Neil Simon that is fast\, verbal\, and rooted in beautifully human flaws. Each night\, a single Seattle hotel room becomes the setting for interconnected scenes inspired by your suggestions\, friends overshare and spiral\, romantic gestures go wrong\, awkward silences stretch\, and small problems become big ones. The comedy comes from character and relationship\, building through sharp dialogue\, escalating tension\, and recognizable\, lived-in situations. Perfect for fans of smart comedy\, relationship humor\, and classic theatrical wit.

From Unexpected Productions

Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Get ready to be surprised\, delighted\, and maybe even see your wildest idea take on a life of its own! Graduate Greenhouse is a Wednesday night showcase featuring alumni from Unexpected Productions Improv School. Each performance highlights a different alumni group\, putting their training to the test with unpredictable scenes\, various formats\, and improvised stories. From long-form storytelling to character work\, these performers explore the boundaries of improv. Jan 7 Market Improv Jan 14 Jan 21 But the fun doesn’t stop there! item.title: Duo Comedy Showcase 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions\, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious\, fast-paced\, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts\, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. \nAt Duos\, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment\, take risks\, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes\, surprising twists\, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. \nWhether you dream of being the next Key & Peele\, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler\, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine\, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend\, form a team\, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo\, email duoshowcase@gmail.com. \nLooking for a midweek serotonin boost? Watching the show is just as fun as performing in it. Grab a seat\, grab a friend\, and discover why Unexpected Productions is the home of Seattle improv.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting\, unexpected\, a little dangerous\, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. \nPart One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance\, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire\, and the improvisers will ignite the stage\, bringing your ideas to life. \nPart Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion\, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song\, a monologue\, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories\, surprising twists\, and big comedic swings. \nLoose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas\, fearless performers\, and the thrill of not knowing what happens next. You light the fuse. They run with it. What they create is entirely up to you.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Spellbound: An Improvised Romantasy
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Epic fantasy meets heart-pounding romance in a one-night-only improvised saga. Spellbound is a bold\, fun\, fully improvised romantasy where epic fantasy and big feelings take center stage. Inspired by books like A Court of Thorns and Roses and Fourth Wing\, our improvisers build a brand-new magical world on the spot using your ideas. Expect dramatic romance\, dangerous magic\, surprising twists\, and characters who fall hard\, mess up spectacularly\, and make very questionable choices in the name of love. It is playful\, passionate\, audience-powered storytelling with high stakes and zero scripts. Every show is a fresh fantasy adventure that only happens once.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Seattle Theatresports 2026
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast\, funny\, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show\, a high-energy\, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show\, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head\, performing games\, stories\, characters\, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds\, decide the winners\, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking\, bold choices\, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show\, unless your parents were super cool.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Youth Clowning with Mick Holsbeke
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Clown Garage at the Improv School Issaquah\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98027

Unleash your silly side! A playful\, youth-friendly class using improv\, physical comedy\, and clown games to build confidence & creativity Get ready to unleash your silly side! This series is all about having fun\, taking creative risks\, and finding the funny in the unexpected. Working as a group to start\, participants will dive head-over-heels into ensemble theater\, improvisation\, physical comedy\, and clown-inspired games\, learning how to turn everyday moments into laugh-out-loud comedy. Whether you’re interested in performing or just want to break out of your comfort zone\, this session is all about embracing play\, thinking on your feet\, and making bold comedic choices. No experience? No problem! Just bring your imagination\, a sense of humor\, and a willingness to get ridiculous. Class Details Dates: Six (6) Monday classes Time: 4:00 pm to 5:20 pm Location: The Clown Garage at Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4 Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill) Maximum students: 12 Ages: 10-15 Cost: $270 Prerequisite: None

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Clown 100: The Foundations of Clown Play with Mick Holsbeke
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Step into the playful world of Clown! Explore presence\, vulnerability\, and joyful failure through ensemble play and laughter Welcome to the world of clowning! This foundational class invites you to step into the playful\, unpredictable\, and joy-filled realm of Clowning with other folks. Through a series of dynamic exercises in presence\, play\, rhythm\, tension\, emotional expression\, and ensemble play\, participants will explore what makes Clown so hilarious and contagious. Embracing vulnerability and the fine art of making mistakes\, we’ll turn failure into laughter and endless comic gold. Whether you’re new to performance or a seasoned artist\, this series will help you build confidence\, take creative risks\, and refine your Clown instincts. Come ready to play\, surprise yourself\, and find the fun in the unexpected! Class Details Dates: Six (6) Mondays classes Time: 7:00 pm to 8:30 pm Location: The Clown Garage at Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4 Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill) Maximum students: 16 Ages: 18 plus Cost: $270 Prerequisite: None

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: NOCHE DE IMPROV EN ESPAÑOL
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Noche de Improvisación en español\, es una noche de jam para los que hablan FLUIDAMENTE español\, que ya sean estudiantes actuales de UP\, exalumnos de UP\, miembros del conjunto y exalumnos\, para jugar juntos y seguir desarrollando sus habilidades de improvisación en español. Noche de Improvisación en español \, está dirigida por Laila Valencia. Los juegos ayudan a los improvisadores a desarrollar habilidades\, desarrollar confianza y fomentar la creatividad. Pero lo mas importante es … ¡es en español y es DIVERTIDO! El espacio es limitado a 16 personas. Fecha y hora: El tercer lunes de cada mes\, de 7 a 9 p. m. Ubicación: 650 S Orcas Suite 101\, Seattle\, WA 98108 Precio: $10 (11.24 con cargos) El Requisito Previo: Unexpected Productions Improv 100 Laila Valencia Nació en Guadalajara Jalisco México finales de los años 70as\, donde vivió toda su infancia y adolescencia\, siempre tuvo interés por las artes escénicas y participó en varios proyectos de teatro y danza contemporanea en la UDG \, a finales de los 90tas\, Se va a vivir a Seattle WA donde conoce la técnica de comedia en improvisación \, de la cual se enamora se inscribe en clases y talleres especializados\,con maestros como Keith Johnstone creador de Theatresports\, Gorilla and Maestro improv shows\, Randy Dixon director artistico de Unexpected Produccions desde hace 20 anos\, talleres con Gary Austin\, Asiste a retiros de improvisacion en Nueva York con artistic new direcctins y desde entonces a participado en 6 festivales internacionales de improvisación\,A Impartido clases para toda las edades\, ahora es miembro de de la compañía más antigua de improvisacionnen Seattle Unexpected Productions\, en la cual continúa su preparación y actúa dos o mas veces por semana \, siempre está trabajando en proyectos diferentes\, forma larga y corta de improv\, contando historias que se inspiradan con las ideas del público.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 ISSAQUAH - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!\, SPRING '26
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Discover improv in Issaquah. Build confidence\, meet new people\, and learn to create stories through fun\, supportive games and scene work. Looking for a fun\, fresh way to connect with others\, spark creativity\, and step out of the everyday routine? Welcome to Intro to Improv at Unexpected Productions Improv School Eastside in Issaquah. Whether you are a working professional hoping to improve communication\, a creative soul searching for a new outlet\, or someone simply wanting more laughter and community in your week\, this class is the perfect place to begin. In this beginner-friendly course\, you will learn the foundational skills of improvisation: active listening\, collaboration\, spontaneity\, and using your own life experiences to create honest\, engaging scenes and stories. Improv is not about being funny\, it is about being present\, supportive\, and open. And yes\, along the way\, you will laugh more than you have in ages. Our community values connection and growth\, and this class is built on those principles. You will work in a supportive group where the guiding philosophy is simple: When your scene partner looks good\, you look good. Together\, we build trust\, confidence\, and creativity\, one scene at a time. Under the guidance of our experienced faculty\, you will learn to: Be a better listener Conquer fears and build confidence Spark creativity Meet new people and make real connections Become a better problem solver Say yes more in your life Step out of your comfort zone Improve public speaking Fail easier (and laugh about it) Discover more about yourself Have an absolute blast This class includes lots of laughter\, lots of stage time\, and zero pressure. Every student begins here. No experience needed. Just bring curiosity and a willingness to try something new. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah\, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau\, East Renton\, Bellevue\, Snoqualmie Valley\, and beyond. Class Details Dates: Six (8) Monday classes Time: 7:00pm to 9:00pm Location: Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4 Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill) Maximum students: 16 Ages: 18 plus Cost: $320 Prerequisite: None Note: Attendance at the first session is mandatory\, as it sets the tone and foundation for the entire quarter. If you know you will miss the first class\, please register for a future session.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Improv Level Up: Turn Games into Scene Magic with Alan Hawkins
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Turn improv games into powerful long-form skills. Build stronger scenes\, relationships and storytelling in this fun 4-week class in Issaquah Many improvisers begin with short-form games\, but hidden inside those fast\, playful formats are powerful tools for deeper scene work. In this four-week class\, we’ll explore how the instincts you already use in games can strengthen your long-form improvisation. By unpacking the mechanics behind classic games\, you’ll learn how to build stronger scenes\, develop clearer relationships\, and create more confident improvised storytelling. Through energetic exercises and supportive coaching\, you’ll discover how skills like listening\, commitment\, pattern recognition\, and emotional connection can expand beyond the game structure and fuel longer\, richer scenes. If you’ve ever wondered how to bridge the gap between short-form fun and long-form storytelling\, this class will help you make that leap. Class Details Dates: Four (4) Tuesdays Time: 7:00 pm–9:00 pm Location: Unexpected Productions – Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar and Grill) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: $270 Prerequisite: Any Improv Class or Experience Meet Your Instructor: Alan Hawkins Alan Hawkins is an improviser from Los Angeles and Chicago with over two decades of performance and teaching experience. A graduate of The Second City Conservatory\, The Second City Musical Conservatory\, iO West\, Comedysportz Chicago\, and The Annoyance\, Alan has performed across the U.S. and internationally with the acclaimed trio Extra Shelves and as a guest with Grand Theft Improv in London. He has taught and directed for numerous programs\, including the Teen Comedy Festival in Chicago. Alan is an ensemble member of Unexpected Productions—America’s longest-running Theatresports company—and the author of “YOU CAN’T LEARN IMPROV FROM A BOOK.” His classes are known for being energetic\, supportive\, and deeply fun. What to Bring This class involves movement\, so please dress comfortably and bring a bottle of water. Cancellation Policy We are unable to offer refunds.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Improv Greenhouse
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Get ready to be surprised\, delighted\, and maybe even see your wildest idea take on a life of its own! Graduate Greenhouse is a Wednesday night showcase featuring alumni from Unexpected Productions Improv School. Each performance highlights a different alumni group\, putting their training to the test with unpredictable scenes\, various formats\, and improvised stories. From long-form storytelling to character work\, these performers explore the boundaries of improv. Jan 7 Market Improv Jan 14 Jan 21 But the fun doesn’t stop there! Stick around for a double feature as we roll into Seattle’s longest-running Improv Open Mic: the Duos Open Mic! Starting at 8:00 pm\, this weekly showcase promises even more laughs\, surprises\, and improv magic.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: 700 Level Student Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Witness the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic by 700 level class.\n\n\n\nJoin the advanced students in our 700 class and witness them blossom with new improv skills. Under the guidance of our expert improv teachers\, 700 students are learning new narrative formats and enhancing their skills by working in an ensemble. You get to see the fruits of their efforts by witnessing the creation of believable characters\, inspiring scenes\, and some improv magic.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Improvised Musical Style and Lyrics with Ron Hippe - Issaquah
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Find Your Voice\, Build Your Confidence\, and Create Songs on the Spot! Now at Unexpected Productions’ new Issaquah location\, unlock your creativity in a supportive\, playful\, and joy-filled class designed for anyone who’s ever hummed in the car\, belted in the shower\, or wondered\, “How do musical improvisers make that magic happen?” Whether you’re a seasoned improviser or stepping into musical improv for the first time\, this course welcomes you with open arms—no prior singing experience required. What You’ll Learn This intensive\, dynamic class explores the foundational skills of musical improvisation through hands-on practice and group collaboration. You’ll learn how to: Create songs and stories in the moment Understand classic song forms and how they apply to improv Strengthen your sense of timing\, rhythm\, and rhyme Turn simple prompts into memorable choruses Build lyrics that feel poetic\, emotional\, or downright hilarious Jump confidently between styles—from Broadway to jazz\, pop to chants It’s fun\, supportive\, and incredibly energizing. You’ll be surprised at what you can create with your classmates—often within minutes. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah\, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau\, East Renton\, Bellevue\, Snoqualmie Valley\, Northbend and beyond. Class Details Dates: Four (4) Wednesdays Time: 7:00pm–9:00pm Location: Unexpected Productions – Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar and Grill) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: $270 ($282.29 with fees

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Improvised Musical Style and Lyrics Class with Ron Hippe
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions Improv School ISSAQUAH/EASTSIDE\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98108\, United States

Find Your Voice\, Build Your Confidence\, and Create Songs on the Spot! Now at Unexpected Productions’ new Issaquah location\, unlock your creativity in a supportive\, playful\, and joy-filled class designed for anyone who’s ever hummed in the car\, belted in the shower\, or wondered\, “How do musical improvisers make that magic happen?” Whether you’re a seasoned improviser or stepping into musical improv for the first time\, this course welcomes you with open arms—no prior singing experience required. What You’ll Learn This intensive\, dynamic class explores the foundational skills of musical improvisation through hands-on practice and group collaboration. You’ll learn how to: Create songs and stories in the moment Understand classic song forms and how they apply to improv Strengthen your sense of timing\, rhythm\, and rhyme Turn simple prompts into memorable choruses Build lyrics that feel poetic\, emotional\, or downright hilarious Jump confidently between styles—from Broadway to jazz\, pop to chants It’s fun\, supportive\, and incredibly energizing. You’ll be surprised at what you can create with your classmates—often within minutes. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah\, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau\, East Renton\, Bellevue\, Snoqualmie Valley\, North Bend and beyond. Class Details Dates: Four (4) Wednesdays Time: 7:00pm–9:00pm Location: Unexpected Productions – Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar and Grill) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: $270 Instructor: Ron Hippe Ron Hippe joined Unexpected Productions in 1990 and has performed globally—from San Francisco to London and Vienna. He has appeared on Broadway in the Pulitzer Prize-winning The Kentucky Cycle and worked with Seattle Rep\, ACT\, and Intiman. Ron is also a veteran voice-over artist\, actor\, musician\, and singer-songwriter. His teaching style is upbeat\, encouraging\, and rooted in decades of professional experience. Cancellation Policy: We are unable to offer refunds.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Showcase 2026
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions\, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious\, fast-paced\, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts\, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. \nAt Duos\, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment\, take risks\, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes\, surprising twists\, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. \nWhether you dream of being the next Key & Peele\, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler\, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine\, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend\, form a team\, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo\, email duoshowcase@gmail.com. \nLooking for a midweek serotonin boost? Watching the show is just as fun as performing in it. Grab a seat\, grab a friend\, and discover why Unexpected Productions is the home of Seattle improv.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Clown 200: Reinforcing Your Clown Foundation with Mick Holsbeke
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Clown Garage at the Improv School Issaquah\, 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4\, Issaquah\, WA\, 98027

Deepen your clown craft through presence\, rhythm\, status\, and physical comedy—embracing failure to build confidence\, independence\, and play In the Clown 200 class series\, students will deepen their understanding of the foundations of theatrical clowning. Participants will engage in exercises focused on presence\, rhythm\, tension\, emotional endurance\, character development\, status play\, and physical humor. By embracing failure and mistakes\, students build confidence to take new comic risks\, grow in their comedic abilities\, and gently approach independence in harnessing the elusive state of Clown. Class Details Dates: Six (6) Thursday classes Time: 5:30 – 7:00 PM Location: The Clown Garage at Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street\, Suite 4 Issaquah\, WA 98027 (next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill) Maximum students: 12 Ages: 18 plus Prerequisite: None

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons 2026
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions\, 1428 Post Alley at the Gum Wall in Pike Place Market\, Seattle\, WA\, 98101\, United States

Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting\, unexpected\, a little dangerous\, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. \nPart One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance\, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire\, and the improvisers will ignite the stage\, bringing your ideas to life. \nPart Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion\, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song\, a monologue\, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories\, surprising twists\, and big comedic swings. \nLoose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas\, fearless performers\, and the thrill of not knowing what happens next. You light the fuse. They run with it. What they create is entirely up to you.

From Unexpected Productions

Event details

item.title: Pike Place Summer Art Walk
item.description:
Thursday, June 4, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Pike Place Market, Seattle

Summer art walk at Pike Place Market
